name: CONGENITAL LYMPHANGIECTASIA
definition: CONGENITAL PULMONARY LYMPHANGIECTASIA abnormal development of lungs between 14th-20th week of GA characterized by anomalous DIFFUSE LYMPHANGIOMA proliferation of mainly lymphatic vascular spaces with relentless systemic progression rare benign usually cystic lesion

demographics.Path: subpleural cysts, ectatic tortuous lymph channels in pleura, interlobular septa + along
demographics.Age: usually manifest at birth; 50% stillborn; M = F
demographics.Histo: collection of dilated + proliferated lymph vessels (? hamartoma / benign neoplasm /

discriminating_features: marked prominence of coarse interstitial markings (simulating interstitial edema)

general: marked prominence of coarse interstitial markings (simulating interstitial edema)
bilateral inhomogeneity of the lung parenchyma on T2WI
high SI in pulmonary interstitium on T2WI
chylous effusions in pleural + pericardial spaces
lymphangiographic pooling of contrast material in dilated lymphatic channels / lymph nodes
may have lytic lesion in contiguous skeleton

clinical.Prognosis: with diffuse involvement invariably fatal at < 2 months of age
